perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Payment terms. Each party shall account to the other on a monthly basis in respect of all sales and revenue received, and payments shall be made within 30 days of receipt of a properly valid invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Payment terms. Each party shall account to the other on a monthly basis in respect of all sales and revenue received, and payments shall be made within 60 days of receipt of a properly valid invoice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because it modifies the payment deadline from 30 days to 60 days. This directly contradicts the payment term specified in Section 6.2.1, creating uncertainty about when payments are actually due and creating a breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2.1 For Cause. Either Party may serve Notice to the other Party to terminate this Agreement immediately in the event any material breach of a material provision of this Agreement by such other Party remains uncured 30 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other material breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 30 days to 60 days, directly contradicting the clause in Section 6.2.1 which allows termination for a breach of payment obligation if uncured within 30 days. This creates a direct conflict regarding the timeframe for curing a payment breach and potential termination, leading to an inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2.1 For Cause. Either Party may serve Notice to the other Party to terminate this Agreement immediately in the event any material breach of a material provision of this Agreement by such other Party remains uncured 30 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other material breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2.1 For Cause. Either Party may serve Notice to the other Party to terminate this Agreement immediately in the event any material breach of a material provision of this Agreement by such other Party remains uncured 60 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other material breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the cure period for a breach of payment obligation from 30 days to 60 days. This contradicts Section 4.4, which specifies payments shall be made within 30 days, but then clause 6.2.1 now states that there is 60 days to cure a breach of that obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Payment terms. Each party shall account to the other on a monthly basis in respect of all sales and revenue received, and payments shall be made within 30 days of receipt of a properly valid invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the cure period for a breach of payment obligation from 30 days to 60 days in Section 6.2.1. This directly contradicts Section 4.4, which mandates payments be made within 30 days of invoice receipt. The changed text allows for a 60-day cure period, making the obligations directly contradictory. This represents an inconsistency that would cause confusion regarding the payment terms and enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2.2 In consideration of the Due Diligence Services, ISA shall pay to Rubicon the Due Diligence Fees within 30 days of receipt from Rubicon of an invoice and any previously requested supporting documentation, including time charges.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2.2 In consideration of the Due Diligence Services, ISA shall pay to Rubicon the Due Diligence Fees within 45 days of receipt from Rubicon of an invoice and any previously requested supporting documentation, including time charges.
perturbation.explanation: The payment term for Due Diligence Services has been altered from 30 days to 45 days. This conflicts with Section 6.2.1 that allows termination of the agreement if a payment breach remains uncured for 30 days creating an uncertainty in whether due diligence fees are now subjected to a different time frame for termination in section 6.2.1 or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2.1 For Cause. Either Party may serve Notice to the other Party to terminate this Agreement immediately in the event any material breach of a material provision of this Agreement by such other Party remains uncured 30 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other material breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment term from 30 to 45 days. Section 6.2.1 allows termination if a payment breach remains uncured for 30 days. The change directly contradicts the termination clause by creating uncertainty in the timeframe for a payment breach, thus the 30 day breach period for termination as it applies to the Due Diligence Fees is now unclear creating a direct contradiction, because the Due Diligence Fees are now subjected to a new payment term. This creates an inconsistency between the two sections.
